QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_44

question.text: Assignment of exercised options to option writers is done on:

answer.text: Random basis among all short option positions of the same series at the clearing member level
First-in-first-out basis based on when positions were written
Pro-rata basis based on the size of short positions
Assigned to the writer with the largest short position first

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When option holders exercise their options, the clearing corporation assigns the exercise obligation to option writers using a random assignment method. This ensures fairness - no writer is systematically targeted for assignment. The assignment is done at the CM level first, then the CM assigns to TMs/clients.
**The Trap:** It is NOT FIFO or pro-rata. Random assignment means any writer could be assigned regardless of when they wrote the option or the size of their position.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Assignment mechanics are important for option writers to understand their risk of early/expiry assignment.
**Key Takeaway:** Option assignment = random basis. Any writer can be assigned. Done at CM level first, then distributed to TMs/clients.
